channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Ben Horowitz’s dual support for Trump and Harris says a lot about the Valley’s AI politics
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. I’m Mark Sullivan, a senior writer at Fast Company, covering emerging tech, AI, and tech policy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Marriott settles with the FTC for $52 million over data breaches
item.description: Marriott International has agreed to pay $52 million and make changes to bolster its data security to resolve state and federal claims related to major data breaches that affected more than 300 million of its customers worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors from mines in a western North Carolina community harmed by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dualtronics Breakthrough: Cornell Researchers Revolutionize Semiconductor Tech
item.description: Cornell University and Polish Academy of Sciences collaboration leads to the first-ever dual-sided chip, integrating photonic and electronic devices on a single Gallium Nitride wafer, with potential applications in display manufacturing, radio frequency devices, lasers, and future 5G/6G technologies.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: AMD Ryzen AI Pro 300: Enterprise-Focused Lineup Brings More Powerful AI Chips to Businesses
item.description: AMD announces the Ryzen AI Pro 300, an enterprise-focused lineup with more powerful AI chips for improved collaboration, security, and more.
item.dc:creator: John.Loeffler@futurenet.com (John Loeffler), TechRadar

item.title: Cisco revamps key DevNet sandboxes
item.description: Cisco has expanded its DevNet sandbox environments to make it easier for network professionals to test and develop applications for key enterprise networking technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: sfc bug detected in new Windows 11 24H2 update - Microsoft sure to issue a patch
item.description: The new Windows 11 24H2 update has a new bug affecting sfc. The tool keeps returning false positives. It finds “corrupt files” that require “fixing”, except there’s nothing wrong with the file it has detected.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: This has to be one of the weirdest PCIe cards ever sold — Japanese firm fuses antiquated parallel port with PCIe slot, and promises it is compatible with Windows XP
item.description: Japanese firm fuses antiquated parallel port with PCIe slot and promises that it is compatible with Windows XP.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: IBM: 78% of mainframe shops are piloting, operating AI apps
item.description: AI is reinvigorating the mainframe and causing enterprises to rethink their plans for mainframe modernization.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trials for 2019 kidnapping, killing of Santa Cruz tech entrepreneur divided into four
item.description: Attorneys have argued in court filings that a fair trial was not possible because several of the defendants implicated each other during post-arrest interviews with police detectives, and that transcript redactions would be difficult to untangle for each.
item.dc:creator: Jessica York, The Mercury News

item.title: Waiting for JD: Vance’s appearance at Silicon Valley fundraiser keeps nearby crowd on edge
item.description: Admissions to the event- the host was not clear - were going for up to $50,000 and the cheapest ticket costing $3,000.
item.dc:creator: Caelyn Pender, The Mercury News

item.title: Five days a week in the office? Forget it!
item.description: A few weeks ago, Amazon CEO Andy Jassy announced that all corporate employees, including AWS staffers, will be expected to work in the office five days a week beginning in January. I don’t work for Amazon, but if I did, I’d quit. Period. End of statement.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Here’s what’s new in Office 2024
item.description: Microsoft has just released Office 2024, the new non-subscription version of its office suite. It’s available in three editions: Office Home 2024 for consumers, Office Home & Business 2024 for consumers and small businesses, and Office LTSC 2024 for midsize and large businesses.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How NTT DATA CIO Barry Shurkey takes a people-first approach to IT success
item.description: As CIO at NTT DATA North America, Barry Shurkey is responsible for digital transformation and optimizing the IT roadmap to support the company and its clients.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft’s Drasi: A lightweight approach to event-driven programming
item.description: The Microsoft Azure Incubations team is one of the more interesting components of Microsoft’s hyperscale cloud. It is something between a traditional software development team and a research organization working to build solutions to the problems of distributed systems at massive scale.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: PMI, 프로젝트 성공의 기준 재정립한다 “11월 연구 결과 공개”
item.description: 글로벌 프로젝트 관리 기관 PMI(Project Management Institute)가 프로젝트 성공에 대한 정의와 프로젝트 전문가의 역할에 대한 기존 관념을 바꿀 새로운 연구를 11월 공개한다고 밝혔다. 이 조직의 새로운 정의에 따르면 ‘성공적인 프로젝트란 투입한 노력과 비용 대비 가치 있는 결과를 창출하는 것’이다. PMI는 프로젝트 성공의 개념을 새롭게 정립하는 야심 찬 시도라며, 앞으로 프로젝트 전문가들의 역할 인식에 근본적인 변화를 가져올 것으로 예상했다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Why it’s important to support fintechs in the acceleration age
item.description: In a time where the digital world is shifting beneath our feet, the speed of technological change can feel like it has never been greater. Tech advancements achieve widespread adoption seemingly at breakneck speeds.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Colella, Fast Company

item.title: Tesla is unveiling its long-awaited robotaxi amid doubts about the technology it runs on
item.description: Expectations are high for Tesla’s long-awaited robotaxi unveiling at a Hollywood studio Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Tom Krisher, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Twin boys born conjoined celebrate 1st birthday after separation surgery
item.description: Twin brothers who were born conjoined recently celebrated their first birthday after undergoing successful separation surgery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google scientists share Nobel Prize in chemistry for AI protein work
item.description: Two Google DeepMind scientists shared the 2024 Nobel Prize in chemistry with a US professor for their breakthrough research into proteins.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: TikTok is designed to be addictive to kids and causes them harm, US states' lawsuits say
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia filed lawsuits against TikTok on Tuesday, saying that the popular short-form video app is designed to be addictive to kids and harms their mental health.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Permitting council invests $15M in ‘surge capacity’ for agencies to manage infrastructure boom
item.description: Infrastructure projects supported by the Federal Permitting Council have seen a nearly 18-month reduction in the permitting process. The post Permitting council invests $15M in ‘surge capacity’ for agencies to manage infrastructure boom first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: IoT-driven endpoint proliferation requires secure-by-design principles
item.description: The government needs to push the market in the direction of secure-by-design, including manufacturing, fielding and management of these new endpoints. The post IoT-driven endpoint proliferation requires secure-by-design principles first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Federal Executive Forum Customer Experience Strategies in Government Progress and Best Practices 2024
item.description: Improving customer experience (CX) is a multi-faceted challenge to tackle as needs vary between stakeholders.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: American Water, largest water utility in US, dealing with cyberattack
item.description: The largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States announced Monday that it was the victim of a cyberattack, prompting the firm to pause billing to customers.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Titan's former lead engineer says he felt pressured to get the submersible ready
item.description: The lead engineer for an experimental submersible that imploded en route to the wreck of the Titanic testified Monday that he felt pressured to get the vessel ready to dive and refused to pilot it for a journey several years earlier.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and LISA J. ADAMS WAGNER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Open source isn’t going to save AI
item.description: “You don’t want AI only in the hands of a few companies.” Thus spake Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue, suggesting that open source will come to the rescue of AI. It’s a nice thought, but one that has exactly zero historical precedent.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5 ways companies can use time series forecasting
item.description: Time series forecasting is a powerful machine learning method that leverages historical time-stamped data to predict future events and help reduce uncertainty from business conditions — for example, to accurately predict sales, inventory levels, and even manufacturing data.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Advocacy groups suspend use of 'suicide capsule' pending Swiss criminal probe of woman's death
item.description: Advocacy groups behind a so-called suicide capsule said Sunday they have suspended the process of taking applications to use it — which numbered over 370 last month — as a criminal investigation into its first use in Switzerland is completed.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to stream: Coldplay, an unhinged Charlie Puth, 'Salem’s Lot,' Sarah Paulson and NHL 25
item.description: Coldplay's 10th studio album and the American Music Awards celebrating their 50th anniversary are some of the new television, films, music and games headed to a device near you.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments
item.description: Tim Gribben, the commissioner of the Bureau of the Fiscal Service, said the bureau refined and reframed its progress statement for the first time since 2018. The post Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Civilization 7 makers work with Shawnee to bring sincere representation of the tribe to the game
item.description: MIAMI, Okla. (AP) — Shawnee Tribe Chief Ben Barnes grew up playing video games, including “probably hundreds of hours” colonizing a distant planet in the 1999 title Sid Meier's Alpha Centauri.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, PIX 11
